DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, MAY-17-2021 HEARING HELD RE: PLAINTIFF ANA MORLEY'S CORRECTED VERSION OF THE MARCH 31, 2021, NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY REQUESTS AND IMPOSE SANCTIONS. AFTER THE CONCLUSION OF THE HEARING, THE COURT RECEIVES AN EMAIL FROM PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL INFORMING THE COURT THAT PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL WERE ON THE INCORRECT ZOOM LINK. THE COURT CONTINUES THIS MATTER TO BE HEARD ON MAY 25, 2021 AT 1:30 P.M. MATTER TO BE HEARD BY JUDGE PRO TEM AARON MINNIS. THE JUDGE PRO TEM HAS INFORMED COUNSEL FOR PARTIES OF THE NEW DATE AND TIME VIA EMAIL AND WILL SEND A ZOOM INVITATION DIRECTLY TO COUNSEL FOR PARTIES FOR THE HEARING. JUDGE PRO TEM: AARON MINNIS; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Corrected Version Of The March 31, 2021, Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery Requests And Impose Sanctions
Off Calendar May-18-2021 Continued To May-25-21 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ROBERT I. KLIGMAN, Esq. (Tel: 415-485-6720) for defendant Steven P. Okuhn;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ANA MORLEY's Corrected Version Of The March 31, 2021, Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery Requests And Impose Sanctions

Defendant stipulates to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for defendant authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion is granted, in part. The motion is timely because it was electronically served on March 31, 2021, the last date per the parties' stipulation. See Young Decl Para. 13:15-18. The motion is granted as to requests for production relating to the parties' jointly-titled financial statements; the Macondray residence; the parties jointly-titled accounts, assets, and liabilities; and communications by or between the parties relating thereto. Within 30 days, Defendant shall provide verified, Code-compliant responses and produce documents. Where no documents exist, Defendant shall affirm that a diligent search and a reasonable inquiry has been made. CCP 2031.230. Defendant shall provide a privilege log and counsel for the parties shall promptly meet and confer regarding a protective order.

As to the remaining requests, the motion is denied without prejudice. The requests are overbroad and seek vast information about every facet of Defendant's finances individually, and in some instances the finances of third parties, which is premature given the pleadings are to be amended, the absence of a protective order, and the lack of sufficient meet and confer by counsel to narrow the issues in dispute prior to filing the instant motion. Moreover, a right of privacy exists as to a party's or third party's confidential financial affairs, even when the information sought is admittedly relevant to the litigation. Cobb v. Sup.Ct. (Tleel) (1979) 99 CA3d 543, 550. The party seeking discovery must show a particularized need for the confidential information sought and that the information is directly relevant to a cause of action or defense, i.e., that it is essential to determining the truth of the matters in dispute (Britt v. Sup.Ct. (San Diego Unified Port Dist.) (1978) 20 C3d 844, 859-862), which Plaintiff has not shown. The request for sanctions is denied.

The prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at aaron@minnisandsmallets.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Aaron Minnis; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

***** After the conclusion of the hearing, the Court receives an email from plaintiff's counsel informing the Court that plaintiff's counsel were on the incorrect Zoom link. The Court continues this matter to be heard on May 25, 2021 at 1:30 p.m. Matter to be heard by Judge Pro Tem Aaron Minnis. The Judge Pro Tem has informed counsel for parties of the new date and time via email and will send a Zoom invitation directly to counsel for parties for the hearing. (302/JPT) *****
